channel.title: Drupal Canvas
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Canvas.

item.title: #3572553: Save Multi-Value Prop Configuration for Code Components
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +2/−199 · js +2 · php +0/−199
  • Diff: 94e27a4
  • Discussion: #3572553 · 6 contributors · 73 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The Canvas editor can now correctly save and reload multi-value (array) prop configurations for code components. Previously, selected values for array-type props did not persist after adding a component and returning to its page. As part of the fix, a short-lived server-side normalization workaround that had been added to compensate for incorrectly structured client data was identified as technical debt and removed, leaving the backend clean and free of compensating logic.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The overall feature work landed in two commits. The first (0920a02a) delivered the core persistence changes. The second, MR !742 (94e27a47), is the corrective diff provided here: it removes JavaScriptComponent::normalizePropsSchema() and the call to it inside updateFromClientSide(). That private method had been moving enum, meta:enum, and x-translation-context keys from the array-schema level down into the items subschema, and also stripping placeholder [[]] examples, to work around clients sending malformed prop data. Wim Leers flagged this as directly opposed to the schema work in #3568264 (which added proper array type support to canvas.json_schema.yml) and as pure technical debt. The correct contract is for the client to submit prop schemas that are already valid, with enum/meta:enum nested under items.

Along with the production code removal, the associated testNormalizePropsSchema test method and its providerNormalizePropsSchema data provider are removed from JavascriptComponentTest, as is the now-unused ConstraintViolationsTestTrait use in that class. The only addition in MR !742 is inline comments in the UI serialization function serializeProps clarifying the hasExample guard logic for array versus non-array props, where string arrays require at least one non-empty value.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 10, 2026
  • First commit: March 10, 2026 (28 days later)
  • Last commit: April 28, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Collaboration

During review, wim leers identified normalizePropsSchema() as technical debt that was "literally opposed to" the schema improvements landed in #3568264, which this issue had been blocked on. After the initial commit included the method, tedbow opened MR !742 specifically to remove it, and wim leers gave LGTM before it was committed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3573776: Canvas needs a way for server to send notifications and trigger actions in the client
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal plan
  • Version: 1.1.0
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3573776 · 4 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Canvas now has a server-to-client notification system that lets Canvas extensions surface messages to authenticated users without a page reload. Notifications appear in two places: an Activity Center dropdown (bell icon in the top bar) and as auto-dismissing toast messages in the top-right corner. Five types are supported: processing, success, info, warning, and error. The entire feature is currently gated behind drupalSettings.canvas.devMode.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: Two new tables added: canvas_notifications and canvas_notification_reads.
  • New API: New NotificationService PHP class and GET /canvas/api/v0/notifications / POST /canvas/api/v0/notifications/read REST endpoints for creating and polling notifications from Canvas extensions.

Technical details

The backend stores notifications in two new database tables: canvas_notifications (id, type, key, title, message, timestamp, actions) and canvas_notification_reads (uid, notification_id, timestamp). A NotificationService PHP class handles creation and retrieval. Each notification gets a UUID on creation; an optional key field groups related notifications so that creating a new notification with a given key automatically deletes all prior error, warning, and processing notifications for that key, enabling clean state transitions such as processing -> success or error -> processing on retry.

Two REST endpoints are exposed: GET /canvas/api/v0/notifications returns up to 25 recent non-processing notifications plus all active processing notifications with per-user hasRead state; POST /canvas/api/v0/notifications/read marks a list of notification IDs as read for the current user. A cron hook replaces stale processing notifications (older than 30 minutes) with a timeout error and purges all notifications and read entries older than 30 days.

The frontend uses RTK Query for polling with adaptive frequency: 2 seconds when processing notifications are present, 10 seconds normally, and 5 minutes when the browser tab is not focused. A Redux slice manages UI state. Marking notifications as read uses an optimistic update. The Activity Center is a Radix Popover sorted as: processing at top, then unread errors and warnings (newest first), then everything else chronologically. Toast notifications appear for any notification with a timestamp after page load, auto-dismiss after 15 seconds, and can be dismissed early (which also marks the notification as read).

The design was shaped substantially in the comment thread: justafish proposed the data structure and a full OpenAPI schema including a key-based dismissal mechanism and a separate read endpoint. wim leers pushed back on a dedicated polling endpoint (preferring consolidation with the existing autosave poll) and raised concerns about information disclosure for globally scoped notifications; the final Phase 1 design accepted a dedicated polling endpoint and global-only notifications as acknowledged trade-offs. Notifications in Phase 1 are broadcast to all authenticated users; per-user or per-role scoping is noted as a future enhancement. The implementation was delivered through child issues #3514033, #3550161, and #3574257.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 16, 2026

Collaboration

justafish drove the API design by posting a detailed client-side data structure proposal and a complete OpenAPI schema for both the GET and POST endpoints, which became the basis for the final implementation. wim leers raised substantive concerns: whether polling should be consolidated with the existing autosave endpoint, how cross-session read state should be tracked (UUID vs timestamp), and whether global notifications created an information disclosure risk. Those exchanges directly influenced the scope decisions documented in the issue: a dedicated polling endpoint was kept for Phase 1 simplicity, and per-user or per-role scoping was explicitly deferred.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586959: Validate image prop example URLs in Code Component metadata files
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3586959 · 1 contributor · 7 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Canvas Code Component authors now get an ESLint error if their component.yml example values for canvas.module/image props use relative or root-relative src URLs instead of fully-qualified ones. The error message recommends using a placeholder such as https://placehold.co/600x400. This gives faster feedback during local development rather than waiting for backend schema validation to catch the problem. The fix shipped in @drupal-canvas/eslint-config@0.3.0 and @drupal-canvas/cli@0.14.0.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ESLint rule component-image-example-urls added at severity error to the required config in @drupal-canvas/eslint-config; enforces fully-qualified URLs for canvas.module/image prop example src values in component.yml files.

Technical details

The issue adds a new ESLint rule component-image-example-urls to the required config in @drupal-canvas/eslint-config. The rule lives in rules/component-image-example-urls.js and operates on the yaml-eslint-parser AST. It only activates for files matched by isComponentYmlFile (i.e. component.yml). For each entry in props.properties, hasImageRef checks whether the prop's mapping contains a $ref equal to json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/image, or whether its items mapping does (covering array-of-image props). When an image prop is found, getInvalidImageExampleNodes walks the examples sequence and inspects each image object's src scalar. isFullyQualifiedUrl uses the standard URL constructor and requires both a non-empty protocol and hostname; anything else (relative paths, root-relative paths) produces an ESLint error with a message pointing to https://placehold.co/600x400. The rule is registered at severity 'error' alongside existing rules such as component-dir-name and component-prop-names. The underlying backend schema gap that permits the same invalid values through the JSON schema validator is tracked separately in #3586958.

Upgrade

Update the ESLint config package directly:

npm install @drupal-canvas/eslint-config@0.3.0

Or, if using the Canvas CLI, upgrade to the new release which pulls in the updated config automatically:

npm install @drupal-canvas/cli@0.14.0

After upgrading, any component.yml file that has an image prop example with a relative or root-relative src (e.g. ./hero.jpg or /sites/default/files/hero.jpg) will produce an ESLint error. Replace those values with fully-qualified URLs such as https://placehold.co/600x400.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 26, 2026
  • Committed: April 26, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3574994: Resizable right sidebar for Drupal Canvas
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +282/−121 · js +232/−106 · css +50/−15
  • Diff: d0a85f2
  • Discussion: #3574994 · 7 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The right sidebar in Drupal Canvas can now be resized by dragging a handle. Users can adjust the sidebar width to fit their workflow and screen size. The chosen width is remembered across sessions. This improves the editing experience when working with component properties that need more space or when maximizing the preview area.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The implementation replaces the react-resizable-panels library with allotment for both the editor right sidebar and the code editor layout. The new library provides better control over pane visibility and sizing constraints.

In the main editor, the Editor component now wraps the editor frame and ContextualPanel in an Allotment component. The sidebar pane has constraints: SIDEBAR_MIN_PX (320px) minimum, SIDEBAR_MAX_PX (640px) maximum, and a default of 24% width. The pane is hidden when no component is selected in the template editor or when in interactive mode, using the visible prop rather than CSS display none.

The code editor similarly uses nested Allotment components for horizontal and vertical splits. Both layouts persist their sizes to localStorage using new layoutStorage.ts helpers (getLayoutItem and setLayoutItem). These helpers catch storage errors gracefully so the UI falls back to defaults if localStorage is unavailable.

The ContextualPanel CSS changed from a fixed width to flex-based sizing to work within the resizable pane. The layout token --sidebar-right-width was removed since width is now dynamic. Playwright tests were updated to wait for the contextual panel to mount after component selection, as the Allotment pane visibility transitions can delay rendering.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 21, 2026
  • First commit: February 22, 2026 (1 day later)
  • Last commit: April 24, 2026 (2 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • jessebaker (Acquia)
  • mayur-sose

Collaboration

balintbrews made their first commit to the Canvas project with this feature. lauriii opened the issue and provided guidance on the implementation approach.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586342: Symmetrically translatable component trees, STEP 1: introduce `ComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGeneratorInterface`
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 20 files, +1006/−6 · php +966/−6 · yml +24 · md +16
  • Diff: 4e6093e
  • Discussion: #3586342 · 2 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

This is an internal architectural change with no user-facing effect. It lays the groundwork for symmetrical translation of component tree inputs on multilingual Canvas sites. Once the follow-up issues (#3582478 for config-defined trees and #3583684 for content-defined trees) land, editors will be able to translate individual component inputs in both config entities and content entities using a consistent, per-input translatability model.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentSourceBase::getExplicitInputDefinitions() changed from protected to public; subclasses that override it as protected will break.
  • New API: New ComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGeneratorInterface with getConfigSchemaMapping() and refineForInstance(), new inputs_config_schema_generator parameter on #[ComponentSource] attribute, new static ComponentInputs::resolveConfigSchemaMapping(), and new ComponentInputs::getTranslatableInputKeys().

Technical details

The core problem is that translating component instance inputs requires knowing which of those inputs are actually translatable, and that knowledge must be consistent between config entity translation (driven by configuration schema discovered by config_translation) and content entity translation (driven by content_translation). Before this change there was no single source of truth for that.

The solution introduces ComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGeneratorInterface with two methods: getConfigSchemaMapping(ComponentSourceInterface $component_source) generates a config schema mapping for all inputs of a component version, and refineForInstance(array $mapping, array $actual_inputs, string $component_id, string $component_version) adjusts that mapping per-instance (for example, removing translatable: true from an input that is populated by a non-static prop source rather than direct user input).

A new inputs_config_schema_generator constructor parameter is added to the ComponentSource attribute, accepting an FQCN implementing this interface. It defaults to FallbackComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator, which emits type: ignore for all inputs and marks nothing as translatable. Three concrete implementations are provided:

  • FallbackComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator: the default, produces type: ignore for every input key with no translatability.
  • BlockComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator: reads the existing block.settings.* config schema via TypedConfigManagerInterface, strips provider, id, and context_mapping, and returns the native block settings mapping. Block settings are plain values so refineForInstance() is a no-op.
  • GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator: inspects the normalized JSON schema prop shapes from GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentSourceBase::getExplicitInputDefinitions() and marks a prop as translatable: true when its shape is type: string (with no enum) and its format is absent or one of iri, iri-reference, uri, uri-reference, or when contentMediaType is text/html. In refineForInstance(), any prop whose stored value is not a StaticPropSource has its translatable key removed, because only user-authored static values should be translated.

The static method ComponentInputs::resolveConfigSchemaMapping(string $component_id, string $component_version, array $actual_inputs) is the single point of truth: it loads the Component config entity, resolves the generator class from the plugin definition, instantiates it via ClassResolverInterface (to support container injection as in BlockComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator), and calls getConfigSchemaMapping() then refineForInstance(). A new instance method ComponentInputs::getTranslatableInputKeys() calls resolveConfigSchemaMapping() and filters to keys whose schema definition has translatable: true (using an ephemeral Mapping object to resolve inherited translatability from core's config schema type hierarchy).

BlockComponent and both GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUx-based sources (JsComponent and the SDC source) have their #[ComponentSource] attributes updated to specify their respective generator class.

GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentSourceBase::getExplicitInputDefinitions() is promoted from protected to public so that GeneratedFieldExplicitInputUxComponentInstanceInputsConfigSchemaGenerator can call it from outside the class hierarchy.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 22, 2026
  • Committed: April 23, 2026

Key contributors

  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • tedbow (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585978: Include page paths and descriptions in the CLI push/pull commands
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +116/−12 · js +49/−7 · php +48/−4 · yml +10 · json +9/−1
  • Diff: 28245af
  • Discussion: #3585978 · 2 contributors · 7 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas CLI push and pull commands now sync page path aliases and meta descriptions in addition to title and components. Previously, when pushing a local page spec, the path was always taken from the remote record and description was never transferred at all. After this change, both fields are stored in the local spec file and round-trip correctly through the API.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: The authored page spec JSON schema now requires a path field alongside title and elements.
  • New API: The Page interface and ApiService.createPage/updatePage gain a required description: string field; path is now string (not string | null) in those method signatures.

Technical details

The issue builds on #3575874, which first added pages to the CLI commands. Two fields were missing from that initial implementation: path and description.

On the TypeScript CLI side, the Page interface gains a description: string field. pageToAuthoredSpec is refactored to build a shared meta object containing uuid, title, path, and description, which is spread into both the empty-components and populated-components return values. preparePages reads description and path from the local JSON spec (both optional, defaulting to ''). pushPages now passes description and page.path (from the spec file) when calling ApiService.createPage and ApiService.updatePage, replacing the previous behaviour of using remotePage.path on update and a hard-coded empty string on create. The path parameter type in both API service methods also tightens from string | null to string. The JSON schema (page-spec.schema.json) adds path to the required array and adds an optional description string property. parsePageSpec adds both path and description to its allowedTopLevelKeys set.

On the Drupal PHP side, ApiContentControllers adds description to the field loop that checks edit access during PATCH handling and passes it through on POST entity creation. A new private method normalizeWithMetadataAndComponents wraps the existing normalizeWithComponents, appending the description field value from the entity (guarded by a hasField check and a view access check) and moving links to the end of the array. All three controller actions (GET single, PATCH, POST/duplicate) switch from normalizeWithComponents to normalizeWithMetadataAndComponents. The OpenAPI spec adds description (type string, "The meta description.") to the page response schema and to the example payloads.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 21, 2026
  • Committed: April 21, 2026

Key contributors

  • wotnak (Smartbees)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585854: Update JS packages
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +3/−2 · json +3/−2
  • Diff: 2d3e7e4
  • Discussion: #3585854 · 1 contributor · 9 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

This change updates JavaScript dependencies in the Canvas project to eliminate 20 npm audit vulnerabilities (1 low, 12 moderate, 6 high, 1 critical). After the update, npm audit reports zero vulnerabilities. Site owners running or developing with Canvas are not affected at runtime; this is a maintenance update to keep the project's toolchain secure.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The issue tracked 20 npm audit vulnerabilities in the Canvas monorepo's JavaScript dependency tree. The fix bumps numerous packages in package-lock.json and tightens version constraints in package.json files for packages/cli and packages/workbench. Notable updates include: axios from ^1.9.0 / ^1.7.8 to ^1.15.1 (with a corresponding proxy-from-env major bump from 1.1.0 to 2.1.0, a transitive dependency), follow-redirects 1.15.11 to 1.16.0, dompurify 3.3.2 to 3.4.0 (also dropping its Node 20 engine constraint), lodash and lodash-es 4.17.23 to 4.18.1, brace-expansion v1 1.1.12 to 1.1.14 across multiple dependents, astro 5.18.0 to 5.18.1, vite 6.4.1 to 6.4.2 and 7.3.1 to 7.3.2, hono 4.12.8 to 4.12.14, and several others. A yaml: ^2.8.3 override was added to the root package.json to deduplicate the four separate yaml 1.x shims that had been installed under oas-linter, oas-resolver, oas-validator, and swagger2openapi, as well as the yaml-language-server pinned 2.7.1 entry; all are now satisfied by the hoisted yaml 2.8.3.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 20, 2026
  • Committed: April 21, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • mglaman (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585314: Add logo.url and favicon.url to site data for code components
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +181/−4 · php +110/−1 · yml +57/−3 · js +14
  • Diff: 648bb9d
  • Discussion: #3585314 · 2 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The Canvas module's getSiteData() function now exposes the active theme's logo and favicon through a new themeAssets field. Code components can access getSiteData().themeAssets.logo.url and getSiteData().themeAssets.favicon.url (plus favicon.mimeType) without reaching outside Canvas's data layer. This is a data-layer addition with no admin UI changes.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New themeAssets field on the SiteData type returned by getSiteData(), exposing logo.url, favicon.url, and favicon.mimeType from the active theme; backed by the new canvas/canvasData.v0.themeAssets Drupal library and CodeComponentDataProvider::getCanvasDataThemeAssetsV0().

Technical details

Canvas exposes Drupal data to JavaScript code components through the drupalSettings.canvasData.v0.* namespace, with each data slice backed by a dedicated Drupal asset library and a PHP provider method. Before this change, getSiteData() covered branding (site name, slogan, home URL) and baseUrl but had no canonical way to supply theme logo or favicon paths.

The fix adds a new slice following the existing pattern:

PHP (CodeComponentDataProvider): getCanvasDataThemeAssetsV0() reads theme_get_setting('logo.url'), theme_get_setting('favicon.url'), and theme_get_setting('favicon.mimetype'), returning them under the v0.themeAssets key.

PHP (ComponentSourceHooks): 'canvas/canvasData.v0.themeAssets' => 'getCanvasDataThemeAssetsV0' is added to ASSET_LIBRARY_METHOD_MAPPING. The jsSettingsAlter() conditional block populates the setting only when themeAssets.logo.url is still NULL, preserving any value set earlier and allowing downstream overrides via array_replace_recursive.

Asset library (canvas.libraries.yml): A new canvasData.v0.themeAssets library entry is declared with drupalSettings defaults (logo.url: null, favicon.url: null, favicon.mimeType: null). The parent canvasData.v0 library's dependency list and the canvas.js_component.* config schema Choice constraint are both updated to include v0.themeAssets (with the full list alphabetically ordered, which also corrects the previous ordering of v0.jsonapiSettings).

TypeScript (drupal-utils.ts): A ThemeAssets interface is defined with logo: { url: string } and favicon: { url: string; mimeType: string }. SiteData gains a themeAssets: ThemeAssets field, and getSiteData() reads window.drupalSettings?.canvasData?.v0?.themeAssets with a safe fallback. The DrupalSettings type definition is also extended to match.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 16, 2026
  • Committed: April 17, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582921: Support OAuth 2.0 authorization_code login flow for Canvas CLI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 18 files, +1447/−52 · js +1343/−47 · md +102/−5 · json +1 · txt +1
  • Diff: d1fd817
  • Discussion: #3582921 · 2 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The Canvas CLI gains an interactive browser-based login command, canvas login, so site administrators and developers can authenticate as their own Drupal user instead of using a shared service-account secret. Running npx canvas login --site-url https://example.com opens a browser, prompts the user to log in to Drupal, and stores the resulting tokens locally. Subsequent canvas push and canvas pull commands use those stored tokens automatically, with no CANVAS_CLIENT_ID or CANVAS_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables required. A matching canvas logout command removes the stored credentials. To use this flow, a Consumer in Drupal admin must have the Authorization Code grant type enabled, PKCE checked, and http://localhost:4444/callback registered as a redirect URI.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The implementation adds three new files to the packages/cli workspace. src/services/auth.ts handles the full OAuth 2.0 flow: discoverAuth() fetches /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource with an X-Canvas-CLI: 1 header to obtain the authorization server base URL and reads the X-Consumer-ID response header to auto-detect the client ID; if that endpoint returns 404 it falls back to Simple OAuth's conventional /oauth/authorize and /oauth/token paths on the site. It then fetches /.well-known/openid-configuration from the auth server to resolve the final authorization_endpoint and token_endpoint. PKCE helpers (generateCodeVerifier, deriveCodeChallenge via SHA-256, generateState) produce the code verifier and S256 challenge. waitForCallback(port) starts a temporary http.createServer listener on http://localhost:PORT/callback with a 5-minute timeout, validates the returned state parameter to guard against CSRF, and resolves with the authorization code. exchangeCode() posts the authorization_code grant with the code verifier to the token endpoint. src/lib/token-store.ts persists the access token, optional refresh token, expiry timestamp, client ID, and token endpoint to ~/.config/drupal-canvas/oauth.json (written as a JSON file with mode 0o600, keyed by normalized site URL) using getTokenEntry, setTokenEntry, and removeTokenEntry. src/commands/login.ts wires these together into loginCommand and logoutCommand registered with Commander. The commit diff also updates the module README to document the Consumer configuration steps (enable Authorization Code grant, check Use PKCE, uncheck Is Confidential, register the callback URI) and adds a note that scopes used with canvas login must have the Authorization Code grant type enabled.

Upgrade

To use canvas login, configure a Consumer in Drupal admin at /admin/config/services/consumer:

  1. Enable the Authorization Code grant type.
  2. Check Use PKCE? and leave Is Confidential? unchecked.
  3. Add http://localhost:4444/callback as a Redirect URI (use a different port if you pass --port).
  4. Enable the Authorization Code grant type on each scope the CLI will request.

Then run:

npx canvas login --site-url https://example.com --client-id YOUR-CLIENT-ID

Tokens are stored in ~/.config/drupal-canvas/oauth.json. Existing client_credentials setups are unaffected.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 2, 2026
  • Committed: April 16, 2026 (13 days later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3578452: Include pages with images in the CLI push/pull commands
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 28 files, +1461/−42 · js +1306/−42 · php +88 · md +38 · yml +24 · json +5
  • Diff: 24bda41
  • Discussion: #3578452 · 3 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The Canvas CLI can now handle pages that contain image props during both push and pull. On pull, the resolved image data (src, alt, width, height) is written to the local JSON spec alongside a new _provenance field that preserves the Drupal media entity reference. A new npx canvas reconcile-media command downloads external images referenced in local specs, uploads them to Drupal, and records the resulting media entity IDs in _provenance. The push command now guards against unreconciled external image URLs and directs users to run reconcile-media first. A new canvas:media:view OAuth scope is also installed automatically on existing sites via a database update.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: Post-update hook canvas_oauth_post_update_0004_media_view_scope creates the canvas:media:view OAuth scope on existing sites.
  • Config change: New canvas_media_view OAuth scope config entity installed by canvas_oauth_post_update_0004_media_view_scope.
  • New API: New reconcile-media CLI command and ApiService.uploadMedia() method for uploading external images to Drupal media entities.

Technical details

The core challenge: json-render page specs store resolved media props (src/alt/width/height) for local rendering, but Drupal's API expects entity references (target_id/target_uuid). The solution is a new _provenance field on AuthoredSpecElement that holds the Drupal-native form of media inputs alongside the human-readable props.

Pull direction (pageToAuthoredSpec): A new extractMediaProvenance helper compares each component node's inputs and inputs_resolved. When a prop in inputs_resolved looks like resolved media (has a src string) and the corresponding entry in inputs is an entity reference (target_id or target_uuid), that inputs value is written as _provenance[propName] in the local spec file.

Push direction (serializeElementMapForServer): A new mediaTransformer is first in the transformer chain in prop-transforms.ts. When _provenance carries a target_id or target_uuid for a media prop, only that key is forwarded to Drupal (the CLI-only source_url is stripped). serializePropsForServer receives the element's _provenance map as a third argument so each prop transformer can access it via the context object.

reconcile-media command (commands/reconcile-media.ts):

  1. Scans all local page specs with collectUnreconciledMediaProps, which calls getUnreconciledMedia per prop. A prop is unreconciled when its src is an absolute external URL or a data: URL.
  2. Deduplicates by URL so each image is downloaded and uploaded only once.
  3. Downloads via downloadExternalMedia (axios with request-filtering-agent) or downloadDataUrlMedia for inline data URLs.
  4. Validates the MIME type against SUPPORTED_IMAGE_TYPES (jpeg, png, gif, webp, avif); SVG is explicitly rejected.
  5. Uploads via the new ApiService.uploadMedia() method, which POSTs multipart/form-data to /canvas/api/v0/media/:mediaType/upload (the endpoint added in #3578449).
  6. Applies results: updates props[propName] to inputs_resolved from Drupal and writes _provenance[propName] = { target_id, source_url }.
  7. Writes updated specs back to disk.

Push guard: preparePages now calls collectUnreconciledMediaProps per page and aggregates the results as pendingMediaReconciliations. If any are found, pushCommand throws before contacting Drupal, instructing the user to run npx canvas reconcile-media.

OAuth scope: A new canvas_media_view config entity and canvas_oauth_post_update_0004_media_view_scope post-update hook ensure the canvas:media:view scope exists on upgraded sites. The CLI's default scope string (DEFAULT_SCOPE) now includes canvas:media:image:create and canvas:media:view.

handleApiError fix: The method's return type was corrected from void to never, which allowed removal of all unreachable throw statements that followed calls to it throughout ApiService.

This work builds on the json-render conversion utilities from #3573996 and the inputs_resolved computed property from #3577654.

Upgrade

Run database updates to install the new canvas:media:view OAuth scope on existing sites:

drush updb

For CLI users: if your local page specs contain image props whose src is an external URL, run the new reconcile step before pushing:

npx canvas reconcile-media
npx canvas push

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 11, 2026
  • Committed: April 16, 2026 (1 month later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585055: Add preview-build command to Workbench
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +1131/−38 · js +1012/−37 · mdx +69 · md +48 · json +1/−1 · txt +1
  • Diff: 53ad1c0
  • Discussion: #3585055 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The @drupal-canvas/workbench package gains a new preview-build CLI command that exports standalone, self-contained HTML preview artifacts for a single component or page without running the full Workbench dev server. This makes it straightforward to generate portable preview output for review pipelines, testing, or downstream tooling. The package was published as version 0.3.0.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New canvas-workbench preview-build CLI command with --component-path/--page-path and --out-dir flags for exporting standalone HTML preview artifacts

Technical details

The issue adds a preview-build subcommand to the canvas-workbench CLI. The entry-point script (canvas-workbench.mjs) was refactored to extract a runChild helper and then branch on the first argument: when it is preview-build, the script delegates to a separate server-side binary at dist/server/src/server/preview-build.mjs; otherwise it falls through to the existing Vite dev-server path.

The core logic lives in the new preview-build.ts module, which exports two public functions: parsePreviewBuildArgs (parses --component-path/--page-path, --out-dir, and --pretty from process.argv) and buildPreviewArtifact (performs the actual export). buildPreviewArtifact accepts an options object and an injected-dependency bag (buildPreviewPayload, discover, resolveConfig, extractMetadata, bundleInteractivePreview) to keep it unit-testable. It stages HTML files to a temporary directory and atomically moves them into --out-dir only after all writes succeed, leaving existing output untouched on failure.

For component targets the output directory contains component-default.html, one component-mock-NN.html per mock variant, and manifest.json. For page targets it contains page-default.html and manifest.json. Each HTML file is self-contained: local asset imports (fonts, images) are bundled inline as data URLs, with binary assets such as WOFF2 and PNG files encoded as base64. manifest.json is target-specific: component manifests include entries.default and entries.mocks; page manifests include only entries.default. Entry path values are relative to the export directory so the bundle is portable. The command prints a JSON payload to stdout with request, target, renderMode (interactive), outDir, manifestPath, summary counts, warnings, and errors.

The client entry point (main.tsx) gained ensurePreviewRuntimeGlobals(), which sets window.React, window.drupalSettings.canvasData.v0, and window.drupalSettings.canvasData.v0.baseUrl to safe defaults when they are absent. This ensures the standalone exported HTML files have the runtime globals that Canvas components expect even outside a live Drupal site.

Path constraints are enforced: --component-path must resolve to a file inside the configured componentDir and --page-path must resolve inside pagesDir, because preview discovery, mocks, and @/ module resolution are all scoped to those roots. Attempting to target a path outside those roots produces an artifact_build_failed error that names the configured directory in its message.

Documentation was added in the Workbench README (a new "Preview build command" section in the configuration reference) and as a new standalone user-docs page at preview-build/.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 15, 2026
  • Committed: April 16, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3583916: Create a new command for adding symlinks for agentic skills after repository has been scaffolded
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +79/−4 · js +58/−4 · md +21
  • Diff: b190c7a
  • Discussion: #3583916 · 2 contributors · 7 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The @drupal-canvas/create CLI now includes an agents subcommand that lets developers set up AI agent compatibility symlinks in an existing project, not just during initial scaffolding. Previously, if agent setup was skipped at scaffold time or a developer wanted to add support for additional agents later, there was no way to do it without rescaffolding. Running npx @drupal-canvas/create@latest agents (optionally with a comma-separated agent list or none) fills that gap.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New agents subcommand in @drupal-canvas/create: npx @drupal-canvas/create@latest agents [agents]

Technical details

The core problem was that setupAgentSkills and the interactive agent-selection prompt were only reachable through the main create flow. The fix introduces a standalone agentsCommand function in a new src/agents.ts file, registered on the commander program instance in index.ts via agentsCommand(program). It accepts an optional [agents] argument (comma-separated list or "none"), resolves the project directory from process.cwd(), and delegates to the existing setupAgentSkills and parseAgentSelection helpers. To support context-appropriate error messages when called from the new command, parseAgentSelection gains an optional label parameter (defaulting to '--agents') that replaces the previously hard-coded string in all three error paths. Two smaller UX improvements landed alongside: setupAgentSkills now logs an informational message when no .agents/skills directory is found (previously it silently returned), and defaultPromptForAgents emits an empty p.log.message('') to add visual spacing before the multiselect prompt.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 9, 2026
  • Committed: April 13, 2026 (4 days later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582865: Include Pages in the CLI validation command
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 7 files, +384/−26 · js +373/−17 · json +11/−9
  • Diff: 1e4001f
  • Discussion: #3582865 · 2 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The canvas validate CLI command now validates pages in addition to components, and the canvas push --include-pages command validates pages before uploading and aborts if any page fails. This makes it easier for developers and AI agents to catch page errors locally before they reach a Drupal site. The change also fixes a long-standing gap where per-component prop validation inside defineComponentCatalog was silently skipped for catalogs with more than one component.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New validatePages() and validateElements() functions in utils/validate-page.ts for validating Canvas page files and their element trees against a component catalog.

Technical details

A new validatePages() async function in utils/validate-page.ts drives the page validation logic. It reads each discovered page file, validates the file structure against page-spec.schema.json using ajv v8 with ajv-formats, then validates the page elements against a component catalog. buildElementsValidationContext() builds that catalog from enabled component metadata via defineComponentCatalog(), exposing a pre-built ElementsValidationContext that validateElements() consumes. validateElements() first checks for disabled components and reports them directly, then converts the AuthoredSpecElementMap to a json-render spec via authoredSpecToComponentTree() and canvasTreeToSpec() and calls catalog.validate().

The drupal-canvas package (bumped to v0.3.2) received two fixes required by this feature. First, defineComponentCatalog() now overrides the default catalog.validate method with one built on a Zod discriminated union keyed on type, so each element's props are checked against its specific component schema. The upstream default fell back to z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()) for catalogs with two or more components, silently bypassing all prop validation. Second, the new stripUriRefFormats() function recursively removes uri-reference and iri-reference format keywords from component JSON schemas before they are passed to fromJSONSchema(). Without this, fromJSONSchema maps those formats to z.url(), which rejects relative URLs. The stripped paths are re-validated with new URL(current, 'http://localhost') to accept relative URLs. A synthetic canvas:component-tree entry is also registered in the catalog to handle multi-root trees produced by canvasTreeToSpec().

In the validate command, results are now typed as Result[], each entry carrying an itemType field ('Component' or 'Page'). The reportResults call uses 'Item' as the label, and the underlying reportResults utility was fixed to use headers.length - 1 for the wrapping column rather than a hardcoded index of 2. In the push command, a validation spinner and a call to validatePages() are inserted before the page push loop; if any page fails, an error is thrown and the push is aborted. The discoverCanvasProject call in validate now passes pagesRoot: config.pagesDir.

This issue builds on page push/pull support added in #3575874 and the local page file format established by #3582632.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 2, 2026
  • Committed: April 13, 2026 (11 days later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3578449: Add an unstable (v0) "canvas_external_api" POST route for uploading an image blob and getting back the ID and UUID of the created media entity
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +831/−9 · php +708 · yml +107 · md +16/−9
  • Diff: 1cf6630
  • Discussion: #3578449 · 4 contributors · 24 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Site owners using Canvas Workbench can now upload images to a Drupal site via the Canvas CLI. A new POST /canvas/api/v0/media/{media_type}/upload endpoint accepts a multipart image upload and creates a file entity and media entity in one round trip, returning the entity ID, UUID, and resolved image prop values ready for use in Canvas page components. This is marked as an unstable v0 API intended for the Canvas CLI rather than the Canvas UI, and it unblocks CLI push/pull for pages that contain image component instances (see #3578452).

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New per-image-media-type OAuth2 scopes (canvas:media:{media_type_id}:create) are created on canvas_oauth install and via post-update canvas_oauth_post_update_0003_media_image_scopes.
  • New API: New POST /canvas/api/v0/media/{media_type}/upload endpoint (unstable v0) accepts a multipart image upload and returns the created media entity's id, uuid, and inputs_resolved fields.

Technical details

The core design decisions were settled by wim leers in comment #2: use Drupal core's FileUploadHandler for all file-handling and security logic (consistent with how REST and JSON:API handle uploads), and bundle file creation and media entity creation into a single request to avoid the state management brittleness of two separate round trips.

The new ApiMediaControllers::upload() method in canvas module handles the request. It validates that the target media type uses the image source plugin, resolves file extension and size validators from the source field definition via FileUploadLocationTrait, wraps the incoming UploadedFile in FormUploadedFile, and delegates to FileUploadHandlerInterface::handleFileUpload(). If upload validation passes, it creates a media entity via entity storage, validates it with TypedDataInterface::validate(), saves it, and returns a 201 JSON response containing id, uuid, and inputs_resolved. The inputs_resolved payload contains src (with the ?alternateWidths query parameter), alt, width, and height, matching the Canvas image prop shape.

The route canvas.api.media.upload is defined at POST /canvas/api/v0/media/{media_type}/upload with access controls _canvas_authentication_required and _entity_create_access: 'media:{media_type}'. The {media_type} path parameter is upcasted to a media_type entity. The route is special-cased in CanvasOauthAuthenticationProvider::applies() so that OAuth tokens are accepted for it (media is not a Canvas-provided entity type and therefore not covered by the existing config-entity routing logic).

OAuth support is provided by a new MediaScopesHelper class in canvas_oauth, which creates a canvas:media:{media_type_id}:create OAuth2 scope (mapped to the create {media_type_id} media permission) for every media type whose source plugin is image. Scopes are created on module install and via canvas_oauth_post_update_0003_media_image_scopes. The follow-up issue #3582492 tracks honoring per-field alt/title requiredness settings.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 11, 2026
  • Committed: April 13, 2026 (1 month later)

Collaboration

Wim leers flagged in comment #2 that file upload is security-sensitive territory, drawing on his prior work on core's file upload unification in #1927648 and #2940383. He required use of FileUploadHandler and argued against two-step uploads (first file, then media entity) as both slower and more fragile. That recommendation directly shaped the final design: a single multipart request that creates both entities in one go. penyaskito then opened the MR and worked through several rounds of security-focused review with wim leers before landing the change.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3583557: Add infinite scroll for pages listing and top navigation
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 12 files, +377/−44 · js +370/−44 · php +7
  • Diff: ef11e19
  • Discussion: #3583557 · 4 contributors · 10 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The Canvas editor's Pages list panel (left sidebar) and top navigation panel previously capped page listings at 50 results. This change adds infinite scroll to both locations: when a user scrolls to the bottom of either list, the next batch of pages loads automatically and appends to the existing list. The feature builds on the offset-based pagination API introduced in #3502691. A secondary sort by entity ID was also added on the backend to guarantee stable pagination when multiple pages share the same creation timestamp.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ContentListResult interface ({ items, totalCount }) replaces the plain ContentStub[] return type of useGetContentListQuery; callers must read data.items instead of data.

Technical details

The backend fix in ApiContentControllers::getContentList adds a secondary .sort($id_key) after the primary .sort($revision_created_field_name, 'DESC'). Without this, pages with identical revision_created values could be returned in varying database-dependent orders across paginated requests, causing duplicates or missing items.

On the frontend the main structural change is in contentApi (RTK Query). The getContentList endpoint return type changed from ContentStub[] to a new ContentListResult interface ({ items: ContentStub[], totalCount: number | null }). Three new RTK Query options enable client-side cache merging across paginated calls: serializeQueryArgs keys the cache by entityType-search only (ignoring offset), merge appends new items to the cache when offset > 0 and replaces them when offset === 0, and forceRefetch triggers a new network request whenever the query args change.

Cache invalidation on delete and create was replaced with manual cache management to avoid losing accumulated items. The delete mutation uses contentApi.util.updateQueryData to optimistically filter the deleted item from all matching cached queries and decrements totalCount, with rollback on failure. The create mutation force-refetches from offset: 0 after success rather than invalidating the Content/LIST tag.

The new usePaginatedContentList hook encapsulates offset state, debounces the search term (300 ms), resets offset to 0 when the search changes, and computes hasMore (true only when not searching and items.length < totalCount). It exposes items, hasMore, and handleLoadMore to callers. Both the Pages and PageInfo containers replaced their direct useGetContentListQuery + useDebounce calls with this hook.

The new InfiniteScrollObserver React component uses IntersectionObserver with a 100 px rootMargin to detect when a 1 px sentinel div enters the viewport, then calls onLoadMore. It is rendered conditionally at the bottom of both the PageList component and the Navigation component when hasMore is true.

Callers that only needed items (useSmartRedirect, UnpublishedChanges) were updated to read data?.items from the new response shape. Cypress tests that previously waited for a re-fetch after deletion were updated to remove those waits since deletion no longer triggers a network request. Playwright tests were added for both panels using page.route to mock paginated API responses, avoiding the need to create 50+ real pages.

Upgrade

If your custom code calls useGetContentListQuery and reads the result directly as an array, update it to read data.items:

// Before
const { data: pageItems } = useGetContentListQuery({ entityType: 'canvas_page' });

// After
const { data } = useGetContentListQuery({ entityType: 'canvas_page' });
const pageItems = data?.items ?? [];

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 7, 2026
  • Committed: April 9, 2026 (1 day later)

Key contributors

  • mglaman (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • hooroomoo (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3580221: Add CLI push/pull for fonts
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 25 files, +3222/−48 · js +3081/−15 · md +115/−15 · php +13/−16 · txt +9 · json +3 · example +1/−2
  • Diff: 040e79d
  • Discussion: #3580221 · 2 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas CLI can now push and pull Brand Kit fonts, allowing font configuration to be version-controlled and deployed like other Canvas assets. When a canvas.brand-kit.json file is present in the project root, canvas push resolves each font family (via unifont for provider-based fonts, or a local file path), uploads the font files to the Drupal site, and syncs the full font list to the global Brand Kit. canvas pull fetches fonts from the global Brand Kit, downloads the files into a local fonts/ directory, and writes local src entries back to canvas.brand-kit.json. Font sync is entirely opt-in: the file must be present for any font operations to run. The administer brand kit permission is no longer hidden from the Drupal permissions page when the canvas_dev_mode module is not installed.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New optional canvas.brand-kit.json file in the project root for font configuration; default CLI OAuth scope now includes canvas:brand_kit
  • Permission change: administer brand kit permission is no longer hidden from the Drupal permissions page when canvas_dev_mode is not installed
  • New API: New createFontsPullTask export in pull.ts and pushFonts / buildFontPushPlannedResults exports in font-push.ts; new TypeScript interfaces FontsConfig, FontFamilyEntry, FontDefaults, FontAxisDefaults, BrandKitConfigFile exported from config.ts

Technical details

The global Brand Kit entity stores font configuration site-wide. Previously it was editor-only; the CLI had no way to version or deploy fonts. This change adds font sync to both push and pull as an optional step gated on the presence of canvas.brand-kit.json in the project root.

Config (packages/cli/src/config.ts): The Config interface gains a fonts?: FontsConfig field. At startup, loadFontsFromBrandKitFile() reads and parses canvas.brand-kit.json (if present) and stores the fonts block. New TypeScript interfaces describe the full font config shape: FontsConfig, FontFamilyEntry (a union of LocalFontFamilyEntry with a src path and ProviderFontFamilyEntry with an optional provider), FontDefaults, FontProviderOptions, and FontAxisDefaults. The BRAND_KIT_CONFIG_FILENAME and BRAND_KIT_GLOBAL_ID constants are exported. The default OAuth scope gains canvas:brand_kit (was canvas:js_component canvas:asset_library; now canvas:js_component canvas:asset_library canvas:brand_kit).

Font push (packages/cli/src/lib/fonts/font-push.ts): pushFonts() validates the config first (via font-validate.ts), then fetches the current Brand Kit to diff against remote. For provider-based families it calls createFontResolver() (a unifont wrapper in font-resolver.ts) and then downloadResolvedFaces() to fetch font files to temp paths. For local src entries it reads the file from disk. Variable font axes are extracted from the font binary using opentype.js (decompressing woff2 with woff2-encoder first) via font-metadata.ts. Axis tags get human-readable display names (e.g. wght → "Weight") for the Brand Kit UI. axisDefaults overrides are clamped to each axis min/max. Only variants not already present on the remote Brand Kit are uploaded; push replaces the remote font set in full so fonts not in config are deleted. All outcomes (create/update/delete/unchanged) are reported. An empty families array clears all fonts on the Brand Kit.

Font pull (packages/cli/src/lib/fonts/font-pull.ts): pullFonts() fetches the Brand Kit via api.getBrandKit(), skips variants already represented in canvas.brand-kit.json (matched by a normalized family\0weight\0style key), and for each new variant downloads the file via api.downloadFile() into a fonts/ directory with slugified filenames. Variable font weight ranges are reconstructed from stored axes (wght min/max). updateBrandKitConfig() merges new LocalFontFamilyEntry objects into the existing file, preserving other top-level keys. createFontsPullTask() in pull.ts wraps this as a PullTask that runs alongside components and global CSS.

OAuth (CanvasOauthAuthenticationProvider): BrandKit::ENTITY_TYPE_ID is added to the list of protected config entity types so the existing config entity REST endpoints (canvas.api.config.get, canvas.api.config.patch, etc.) accept OAuth tokens for Brand Kit operations.

canvas.module: canvas_form_alter() is removed. This hook was hiding the administer brand kit permission entry from the Drupal permissions UI when canvas_dev_mode was not installed. Its removal means the permission is always visible.

New npm dependencies added to the CLI: unifont ^0.7.4, opentype.js ^1.3.4, woff2-encoder ^2.0.0.

Upgrade

If you have CANVAS_SCOPE set explicitly in .env or your environment, add canvas:brand_kit to enable font push/pull:

CANVAS_SCOPE="canvas:js_component canvas:asset_library canvas:brand_kit"

If CANVAS_SCOPE is unset, the new default already includes canvas:brand_kit.

To opt in to font sync, create canvas.brand-kit.json in your project root:

{
  "fonts": {
    "families": [
      {
        "name": "Inter",
        "provider": "google",
        "weights": ["400", "700"],
        "styles": ["normal", "italic"]
      },
      {
        "name": "My Font",
        "src": "fonts/MyFont-Regular.woff2",
        "weights": ["400"],
        "styles": ["normal"]
      }
    ]
  }
}

No changes are required if you do not create canvas.brand-kit.json; the push and pull commands continue to work as before.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 19, 2026
  • Committed: April 8, 2026 (20 days later)

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • hooroomoo (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582709: Provide an endpoint to identify that a push is starting and has stopped
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 12 files, +476/−16 · php +318/−2 · js +84/−14 · yml +74
  • Diff: 4a242b3
  • Discussion: #3582709 · 1 contributor · 12 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas CLI push command now signals the server when a push starts, completes, or fails. This allows the Canvas editor UI to display a notification showing the push is in progress, then update it to show success or failure when the push finishes. The endpoints also dispatch events that other systems can subscribe to.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Three POST endpoints: /canvas/api/v0/push/start, /canvas/api/v0/push/complete, and /canvas/api/v0/push/fail for CLI push lifecycle signaling

Technical details

The Canvas module has an existing notification system that displays messages in the editor UI. This change adds three REST endpoints that the CLI push command calls to signal lifecycle events.

ApiPushController provides three methods that each create a notification and dispatch an event. The start() method creates a "processing" notification. The complete() method creates a "success" notification. The fail() method creates an "error" notification and accepts an optional JSON body with a message field. All three use the same notification key (cli-push), so the existing notification system's KEY_REPLACE_TYPES logic automatically replaces the processing notification when complete or fail is called.

Each method dispatches a PushEvent containing a PushStatus enum value (Started, Completed, or Failed) for extensibility.

The CLI push command in packages/cli/src/commands/push.ts calls /push/start before beginning work and either /push/complete or /push/fail at the end. The calls are best-effort: they are wrapped in try-catch blocks and failures do not affect the push itself. Error handling was refactored to throw exceptions instead of calling process.exit() directly, so the fail signal can be sent in the catch block.

The OAuth authentication provider was updated to recognize these three routes alongside the existing artifact upload route.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 1, 2026
  • Committed: April 3, 2026 (1 day later)

Key contributors

  • mglaman (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • hooroomoo (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582814: Add non-interactive agent selection to @drupal-canvas/create
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +243/−17 · js +221/−11 · md +21/−5 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 11eec2e
  • Discussion: #3582814 · 1 contributor · 6 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The @drupal-canvas/create CLI (version 1.3.0) gains a new --agents flag that lets developers control AI agent compatibility symlink creation without interactive prompts. Previously, the agent setup step always showed an interactive prompt when a .agents/skills directory was present, blocking fully unattended use in CI pipelines or scripts. Now callers can pass a comma-separated agent list (e.g. --agents claude-code,roo) to set up symlinks without prompting, pass --agents none to skip the step explicitly, or omit the flag to retain the existing interactive prompt on TTY runs. On non-TTY runs with no flag provided, the step is silently skipped.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New --agents <list> CLI flag on @drupal-canvas/create for non-interactive agent compatibility symlink control; pass none to skip, a comma-separated list to select, or omit to keep the existing interactive prompt on TTY runs.

Technical details

The change adds a new --agents <agents> option to the commander program in src/cli.js. Parsing is delegated to a new parseAgentSelection() function in src/lib/agent-selection.js, which splits on commas, deduplicates with Set, rejects unknown agent names (via a new isAgentType() guard in agents.js), rejects universal agents that already use .agents/skills, rejects mixing none with other names, and returns undefined when the flag is omitted (preserving the "undecided" state). The resolved AgentType[] | undefined value is threaded through the Context type and passed to createApp(), which forwards it to setupAgentSkills() alongside a new interactive boolean derived from process.stdin.isTTY && process.stdout.isTTY. Inside setupAgentSkills() the logic is: if selectedAgents is a defined array, use it directly and skip the prompt; if it is undefined and interactive is true, call promptForAgents as before; if it is undefined and interactive is false, return immediately without output. An empty explicit selection ([]) prints "No additional agents selected. Skipping compatibility setup." and returns early. The README is also updated to document the new flag and to reflect the currently available predefined templates (acquia-nebula, balintbrews-canvas-starter).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 2, 2026
  • Committed: April 2, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3574410: Hide global regions when editing a view mode other than Full
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +191/−16 · php +129/−9 · js +62/−7
  • Diff: f733d16
  • Discussion: #3574410 · 5 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

When editing a content template for a view mode other than Full (such as Teaser or Card), global regions like header and footer are now hidden from both the editor and preview. These regions do not render when the view mode is displayed on the actual site, so hiding them makes it clearer what the template will actually look like. The editor now shows only the content region for non-full view modes.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

After #3574412 enabled creating content templates for view modes other than Full, the global regions (header, footer, etc.) were still appearing in the editor and preview even though they are not part of the display for those view modes.

The solution adds a shouldIncludeGlobalRegions() method in ApiLayoutController that returns false for ContentTemplate entities whose view mode is not "full". This method is called throughout the controller to conditionally load global regions from PageRegion::loadForActiveTheme(), include editable regions in auto-save validation, and set a #canvas_hide_global_regions flag on the preview build.

In CanvasPreviewRenderer::prepare(), when this flag is set, all page regions except CanvasPageVariant::MAIN_CONTENT_REGION are emptied before rendering.

On the frontend, the component context menu checks whether global regions exist before showing the "Move to region" option. The viewport sets minimum height to 0 for non-full view modes so the preview size matches the actual content rather than requiring a minimum page height.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 18, 2026
  • Committed: March 31, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • pameeela (Technocrat)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • jessebaker (Acquia)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • justafish (Acquia)

Collaboration

Wim Leers initially suggested either server-side changes or a focus mode similar to #3497648. Jesse Baker outlined a longer-term vision for an isolated editor frame similar to no-code component editing, but acknowledged focus mode might be a good compromise. Lauriii implemented the simpler approach of just hiding the regions, explaining this was an intentional product decision to ship the core value efficiently rather than implementing more advanced inline editing capabilities. After Wim raised concerns about preview accuracy being broken, they clarified via direct message that the implementation does preserve page-level markup and CSS. Follow-up #3582608 was created to address potential preview accuracy improvements.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3575874: Include Pages in the CLI push/pull commands
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 27 files, +1903/−37 · js +1851/−30 · md +31/−7 · php +9 · json +6 · yml +6
  • Diff: 96a8b98
  • Discussion: #3575874 · 3 contributors · 12 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The CLI tool's pull and push commands can now transfer Canvas Pages in addition to components and global CSS. Pages are opt-in via the --include-pages flag or CANVAS_INCLUDE_PAGES environment variable. Pages are stored locally as JSON files containing a component tree. The CLI converts between the local format and Canvas's API format, including resolving component versions that may have changed after pushing components.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New CANVAS_INCLUDE_PAGES environment variable (accepts true/false, 1/0, yes/no)
  • Permission change: New OAuth scopes canvas:page:create, canvas:page:read, and canvas:page:edit required when pages are enabled
  • New API: New --include-pages option for pull and push commands to include Canvas pages

Technical details

Canvas pages require component_version for each component in their tree, but the json-render library doesn't track versions and versions can change after pushing components. The CLI now fetches active component versions from /canvas/api/v0/config/component after pushing components and before pushing pages. This required adding the Component entity type (in addition to JavaScriptComponent) to the OAuth-protected config entities in CanvasOauthAuthenticationProvider.

Pages are stored locally in an "authored spec" format with a slots object for better editing ergonomics, rather than the API's flat array with parent_uuid and slot properties. The pageToAuthoredSpec() and authoredSpecToComponentTree() functions handle conversion between formats.

When pulling pages, the CLI matches local files by UUID and updates existing files. When pushing, pages without a UUID or with a UUID not found remotely are created as new pages. The server-assigned UUID is written back to the local file after creation. Pages containing non-JS components (like SDC components) are skipped during pull with a validation error.

The feature defaults to disabled to avoid requiring additional OAuth scopes for users who don't need page management.

Upgrade

To enable page push/pull, configure your OAuth consumer to include the page scopes and use the --include-pages flag:

npx canvas pull --include-pages
npx canvas push --include-pages

Or set the environment variable:

CANVAS_INCLUDE_PAGES=true npx canvas pull

Update your OAuth consumer scopes in the Drupal admin UI to include canvas:page:create, canvas:page:read, and canvas:page:edit.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 26, 2026
  • Committed: March 31, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • mglaman (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
